title: pilea pumila var. hamaoi
intro: Pilea pumila var. hamaoi, also known as creeping Charlie or ground ivy, is a type of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It is often used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pilea pumila var. hamaoi include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural antacid. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into a tea or infused oil to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant require further research.
